budgetEvidence.hardwareTimingRationale: An NTSC NES frame publishes retained OAM at Video.WaitVBlank() before this sample runs shots.Draw(), shots.Request(), and shots.ProcessRequests(). A shot accepted after Draw cannot enter shadow OAM until the following Draw and is legally published at the next VBlank, so the retained-OAM contract allows two sampled frames without reordering Draw or request processing.
budgetEvidence.productionTraceRationale: The production timer requests a shot every ten ticks; the NES two-slot hero pool must preserve the same drop, despawn, reuse, and retained-OAM contract. GCP-2.1 records the two-frame draw-after-request phase contract so target addressing optimizations do not depend on accidental slower timing.
